title: Project Manager International Product Management (f/m/d)
required_skills: Very good English skills (written and spoken)
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills
Proactive team player, ability to manage multiple topics in parallel
matched_skills: Project management experience (international/cross-functional) — HSBC PMO 6-workstream programme, Hapag-Lloyd agile coordination
Communication and stakeholder management — explicit CV skill, C-suite reporting, 300+ attendee events
Structured, analytical mindset — budget variance dashboards, KPI frameworks, sprint reviews
Power BI (PL-300 certified) — bonus skill, exact match
Managing multiple topics in parallel — multi-workstream PMO delivery
missing_skills: Out-of-Home (OOH) logistics experience — no parcel locker / PUDO / last-mile OOH background
German at 'good' level — candidate holds B1 (intermediate), JD expects conversational/working proficiency
Product management or portfolio ownership experience — JD emphasises strategic product positioning, candidate background is ops/PMO-side
core_responsibilities: Drive development and strategic positioning of the Out-of-Home (OOH) portfolio within GLS Group
Manage international OOH projects end-to-end from concept to implementation
Own existing OOH services including quality monitoring and continuous improvement
Identify and implement optimisation and growth opportunities across products and services
Act as central interface between internal departments, country organisations, external partners, and management
recommended_emphasis: Highlight Hapag-Lloyd logistics context prominently — directly signals industry fit
Lead with HSBC cross-functional PMO scope (6 workstreams, USD 8M) to demonstrate international project ownership
Call out Power BI PL-300 certification explicitly — JD lists it as a plus and candidate has a strong differentiator here
Reframe stakeholder coordination bullets to emphasise acting as 'central interface between departments, countries, and management' — mirrors JD language
Mention any exposure to last-mile, parcel delivery, or partner network topics if applicable — to partially address OOH gap
Where possible, demonstrate product thinking (not just coordination): quality monitoring, improvement cycles, growth identification
Use German in cover letter opening line if confident at B1 — signals awareness of language requirement; switch to English thereafter
